vulnerabilities.justification: FHIR API uses UriComponentsBuilder for redirect URLs in SMART on FHIR launch. Attacker-controlled redirect_uri parameter is vulnerable.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: High — could redirect clinician to phishing page after SMART launch, stealing OAuth tokens with PHI scope.
vulnerabilities.action_statement: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.5+. Add redirect_uri validation against registered client list.

vulnerabilities.justification: component_not_present — Scheduling service uses Spring WebFlux, not Spring MVC. UriComponentsBuilder not used in request processing.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: None — component not in execution path.

vulnerabilities.justification: FHIR R4 API deserializes patient-submitted JSON (FHIR resources). Deeply nested JSON could cause stack overflow DoS.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: Medium — DoS only, no PHI exposure. But availability impact to clinical workflows.
vulnerabilities.action_statement: Configure Jackson with max nesting depth of 100. Add request body size limits.

vulnerabilities.justification: AKS cluster uses containerd 1.7.13 which includes the fix. However, need to verify all node pools are updated and no legacy nodes exist.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: Potentially critical — container escape on PHI-processing nodes.
vulnerabilities.action_statement: Verify all AKS node pools running containerd >= 1.7.14. Force node image update.

vulnerabilities.justification: component_not_present — Platform uses Azure Service Bus and Event Hub, not Apache ActiveMQ. No ActiveMQ dependency in any service.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: None — ActiveMQ not used in architecture.

vulnerabilities.justification: vulnerable_code_not_in_execute_path — Base images use Ubuntu 22.04 LTS which uses xz-utils 5.2.5 (not affected). Verified via SBOM scan of all container images.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: None — xz-utils version is not vulnerable.

vulnerabilities.justification: Spring Security authorization bypass could allow unauthenticated access to patient demographics API if custom AuthenticationTrustResolver is misconfigured.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: High — unauthorized PHI access violating HIPAA 164.312(a)(1).
vulnerabilities.action_statement: Upgrade Spring Security to 6.2.3+. Review AuthenticationTrustResolver configuration.

vulnerabilities.justification: gRPC dependency updated to 1.62.0 which includes HTTP/2 Rapid Reset mitigation. AKS ingress controller also patched.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: Previously high (DoS to clinical workflows). Now mitigated.

vulnerabilities.justification: Genomics service uses cryptography 42.0.4 for PKCS12 certificate handling in DICOM mutual TLS. Malicious PKCS12 file could crash the service.
vulnerabilities.impact_statement: Medium — DoS to genomics pipeline. No PHI exposure.
vulnerabilities.action_statement: Upgrade cryptography to 42.0.5+. Add input validation for uploaded certificates.
